book: Atomic Habits Tiny Changes, Remarkable Results An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones - James Clear.kfx

issues.description: Cover title is truncated — 'An Easy & Proven Way to' is visible but the remainder of the subtitle ('Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones') is cut off entirely. The lower half of the cover is a blank beige area with no subtitle completion.
issues.suggestion: Regenerate the cover image or adjust the cover template to ensure the full title and subtitle are rendered within the viewable area.

issues.description: The two-tone background (tan upper block, cream lower block) creates an awkward visual split that makes the cover look unfinished where the title text ends.
issues.suggestion: Verify the source cover asset includes the full subtitle and that the crop/resize step does not clip content.

issues.description: The decorative image at the top of the title page renders as a scattered field of grey dots rather than a recognizable graphic (likely an atom or habit-loop illustration). The source image has degraded significantly.
issues.suggestion: Check the source image asset embedded in the original file and ensure it is embedded at sufficient resolution. Re-run conversion with higher image quality settings.

issues.description: The hierarchy between 'ATOMIC HABITS' (large), the subtitle block, 'JAMES CLEAR', and 'Tiny Changes, Remarkable Results' is slightly ambiguous — 'Tiny Changes, Remarkable Results' appears below the author name in a bold serif that competes visually with the subtitle above.
issues.suggestion: Review stylesheet to ensure the tagline below the author name is clearly subordinate in weight and size.

issues.description: The copyright notice runs 'Copyright © 2018 by James Clear Penguin supports copyright.' — the sentence break between the copyright line and the publisher boilerplate is missing, causing two distinct sentences to merge into one without punctuation separation.
issues.suggestion: Inspect the paragraph delimiter extraction for the copyright page; ensure a line break or paragraph separator is preserved between 'James Clear' and 'Penguin supports copyright.'

issues.description: Multiple distinct paragraphs on the copyright page are merged into a single undifferentiated block. The ISBN line, the internet-addresses disclaimer, and the version stamp are not separated by proper paragraph breaks — they run on continuously.
issues.suggestion: Preserve paragraph-level markup from the source during extraction; do not strip intermediate newlines on legal/front-matter pages.

issues.description: 'AN IMPRINT OF PENGUIN RANDOM HOUSE LLC' and '375 Hudson Street New York, New York 10014' are styled as bold headings, which is slightly unconventional but may reflect the source; not penalized heavily as it appears intentional.
issues.suggestion: Verify against source to confirm these are styled as headers in the original; if not, demote to body text styling.

issues.description: The chapter opening contains a running-header bleed: 'The Role of Family and Friends in Shaping Your Habits' appears as a standalone line of body text immediately after the chapter heading, before the story begins. This is a extracted running header that was not filtered.
issues.suggestion: Add a running-header suppression step in the extraction pipeline to remove repeated chapter-title lines that appear at the top of body pages.

issues.description: 'N 1965' with '1965' rendered as a superscript appears at the start of the chapter narrative. This is a corrupted rendering of 'IN 1965' — the letter 'I' was dropped and '1965' was incorrectly tagged as a superscript.
issues.suggestion: Audit footnote/superscript detection logic; years embedded in narrative prose should not be converted to superscripts. Restore 'IN 1965' as plain body text.

issues.description: Chapter heading '9: The Role of Family and Friends in Shaping Your Habits' is clearly formatted and visually distinct. Minor note: the chapter number prefix format ('9:') is functional but slightly informal compared to typical typeset chapter headings.
issues.suggestion: No action required unless style guide mandates a different chapter number format.

issues.description: The final italic passage 'We are musicians. We are cyclists. The shared identity begins to' breaks mid-sentence at the bottom of the page, which is a normal page-break orphan but the italic styling appears to end abruptly mid-clause, potentially indicating a missed continuation tag.
issues.suggestion: Verify that the italic span continues correctly onto the following page and that the style tag is not closed prematurely at the page boundary.

issues.description: Em-dash spacing in 'in some way— say, your mutual love' has a space before the em-dash but not after, which is inconsistent. Similarly 'at least, not in any noticeable sense' uses an em-dash with a leading space ('faster— at least') suggesting inconsistent dash normalization.
issues.suggestion: Apply consistent em-dash formatting (either spaced or unspaced) across the document during post-processing.

issues.description: Em-dash formatting is inconsistent throughout the page: 'faster— at least', 'here— when we're discussing', 'rewards— is the ending' all show a space before but not after the em-dash, matching the same pattern seen on page 94.
issues.suggestion: Normalize em-dash spacing globally during the conversion post-processing step.

issues.description: Page begins mid-sentence ('and that the work was worth the effort.') which is normal cross-page flow, but there is no top margin or visual cue distinguishing this as a continuation. This is acceptable for ebook format.
issues.suggestion: No action required; standard ebook body page behavior.

issues.description: Same running-header bleed as page 91: '16: How to Stick with Good Habits Every Day' appears as a duplicate line of body text directly below the chapter heading before the narrative begins.
issues.suggestion: This is a systemic issue — the running-header suppression fix recommended for page 91 will resolve this page as well.

issues.description: 'N 1993' with '1993' as superscript appears at the start of the chapter narrative, identical corruption pattern to page 91 ('IN 1993' with 'I' dropped and year incorrectly superscripted).
issues.suggestion: Apply the same fix recommended for page 91: audit superscript detection to exclude years in narrative context.

issues.description: Chapter heading is clearly formatted and visually distinct. No significant heading issues beyond the above.
issues.suggestion: No additional action required for the heading itself.

issues.description: Raw HTML anchor tags and URL-encoded strings are rendered as visible text throughout the entire page. Strings such as '', '', and 'CHAPTER 15 ' are printed literally rather than being parsed as markup. The entire notes/references section is unreadable as citation text.
issues.suggestion: The HTML-to-ebook conversion pipeline is failing to parse or strip anchor tags in the endnotes section. Ensure the notes section is processed through the HTML parser before rendering; do not pass raw HTML strings to the text layer.

issues.description: URL-encoded percent sequences ('%20', '%3A', etc.) appear as literal text throughout the page instead of being decoded to human-readable characters. This makes citation URLs completely unreadable.
issues.suggestion: Decode percent-encoded URL strings before rendering reference URLs in the endnotes section.

issues.description: The citation entries are not formatted as discrete bibliographic paragraphs — they run together with HTML tag noise making it impossible to distinguish where one citation ends and another begins.
issues.suggestion: After fixing the HTML rendering issue, verify that each citation entry is separated by a proper paragraph break.

issues.description: The page begins with a partial URL line that appears to be a continuation from the previous page without context, suggesting the back-matter section break handling is also malformed.
issues.suggestion: Verify page break and section boundary logic for the endnotes/references back matter section.

summary: Evaluated 8 pages across 1 batch(es). Average page score: 67/100.
